metadata.title: FlowStep — single step within a Flow declaration

description: A single step in a Flow's declared sequence (src/body/flows/registry.py). Frozen dataclass. kind discriminates between AtomicAction and nested Flow references via a single ref_id (vs ProposalAction's XOR pattern of action_id/flow_id). consumes governs the parameter routing whitelist per ADR-033: only keys declared in consumes may be passed to this step from the enclosing flow's context. params is a bare dict (no type parameterization) — a minor type-annotation gap, noted but not a governance gap. Governed vocabulary: step_kind enum in .intent/META/enums.json (ADR-056 D5). Authority: ADR-056 Wave 2 Session 1.

ref_id.description: The action_id or flow_id being referenced, depending on kind. Single discriminated field — contrast with ProposalAction's XOR pattern (action_id/flow_id). e.g. 'fix.imports' (action) or 'flow.fix_code' (flow).

kind.description: Step discriminator. Governed vocabulary: step_kind enum in .intent/META/enums.json. Values: action, flow.

required.description: Whether this step is required for the flow to succeed. Default True. Optional steps may fail without failing the flow.

params.description: Parameters passed to the step at execution time. Typed as bare dict (not dict[str, Any]) — minor annotation gap. Default empty dict.

consumes.description: Parameter routing whitelist (tuple[str, ...] | None). Declared per ADR-033: only keys in consumes may be passed from the enclosing flow context to this step. None means no routing filter. Governs step parameter isolation.
